text.title: vui-iot-integration Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for vui-iot-integration as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Claude readily invents device capabilities (e.g., "set the lock to 50%"); validate against the actual capability schema.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generated routines may reference nonexistent device states; always match generated capabilities against a real schema.

failure-mode.description: Generated routines often run in parallel without dependency analysis — locking the door before the user is out, etc.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Smart home workflows have implicit ordering constraints; require explicit step ordering and validate against a constraint model.

failure-mode.description: "Smart home" demos in LLM training often feature unrealistic latency; real cloud round-trips are 1–3 s.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Prompt agents about real latency bounds and test generated routines in a slow-network simulation.

failure-mode.description: Agent will skip privacy considerations (always-on mic, voice transcript retention); add a privacy review checkpoint.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Privacy review is a mandatory gate before deploying voice-control routines in any home environment.

failure-mode.description: Auto-deploying a routine update can lock users out of their own home — require human approval + dry-run.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always require staged rollout and manual approval for critical routines (doors, locks, alarms).

failure-mode.description: Vendor JSON schemas drift quarterly; pin the API version in the prompt and re-validate on every release.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Smart home APIs are not stable; lock schema versions and re-validate generated payloads on every vendor update.
